title: Transitioning to Metropolitan Real Estate Luxury Towers: What Expat Tenants Need to Know Before Arrival
excerpt: Metropolitan Real Estate luxury towers expat guide: arrival relocation, embassy expectations, COI, customs, furnished lease moving Addis Ababa.
content:
Transitioning to Metropolitan Real Estate luxury towers: what expat tenants need to know before arrival

Metropolitan Real Estate luxury towers attract expat tenants expecting international-standard move experience—English coordination, insurance paper, elevator booking, and customs awareness if shipment follows air cargo. Arrival without briefing means jet-lagged argument at gate with incomplete COI.

This guide prepares expat tenants transitioning to Metropolitan luxury towers before arrival.

Pre-arrival checklist expat

  • Lease and compound move form employer HR sometimes facilitates.
  • COI naming Metropolitan management entity exact legal name.
  • Elevator freight slot aligned flight landing plus jet lag buffer.
  • Customs shipment separate track—don't assume same truck day one.
  • Currency and payment method mover contract clear.
  • Emergency contact local fixer or colleague onsite.

Cultural and building norms

Quiet hours; shoe covers lobby; tip culture nuanced—contract price primary; security thorough not personal; WhatsApp coordination norm.

Furnished versus unfurnished

Furnished lease—personal effects air cargo only; unfurnished—local procurement delivery merge move schedule; appliance warranty retain receipts.

Family and school timing

International school calendar; move before term; pediatric records hand-carry; pet import rules parallel track.

Active Movers expat Metropolitan package

English project manager; COI template; airport meet optional; customs liaison referral.

Conclusion

Expat arrival at Metropolitan towers smooth with COI, elevator slot, and shipment tracks aligned before plane lands—not improvised at jet-lag hour.
author: Active Movers Team
